title: Clean up temporary .sassy files
description:
The AssembleCommand writes intermediate .sassy files to the temp directory but never cleans them up.

## Current Code (assemble_command.rb:14-16)
```ruby
sassy_file = File.join(Dir.tmpdir, File.basename(sas_file, '.*') + '.sassy')
File.write(sassy_file, processed_sas)
puts "Temp at  #{sassy_file}"
```

## Options
1. Delete the file after processing
2. Use Tempfile which auto-cleans
3. Only write in verbose/debug mode
4. Remove entirely if not needed for debugging

## Recommendation
Option 3 or 4 - the temp file seems to only be useful for debugging the translation step. Make it conditional on verbose mode.
close_reason: Made .sassy temp file writing conditional on debug mode (verbose flag)

title: Validate field definitions don't overlap
description:
The gem doesn't check if two fields occupy the same columns, which could indicate a parsing error or malformed input.

## Desired Behavior
After parsing, validate that:
- No two fields have overlapping column ranges
- Fields don't extend past the record length (if specified)
- Warn if there are gaps between fields (optional)

## Example
\`\`\`
Warning: Fields overlap at columns 15-20:
  - field_a: columns 10-20
  - field_b: columns 15-25

Warning: Gap detected at columns 30-35 (no field defined)
\`\`\`

## Priority
Low - nice to have for catching errors in complex SAS files

title: Replace escort CLI framework
description:
Escort is an older CLI framework that may not be actively maintained.

## Current State
- Uses escort gem (~> 0.4.0)
- Only uses basic functionality (version, summary, description, action)

## Options
1. **OptionParser (stdlib)** - No external dependency, simple
2. **Thor** - Popular, well-maintained, more features
3. **dry-cli** - Modern, part of dry-rb ecosystem

## Recommendation
OptionParser - the CLI is simple enough that stdlib is sufficient, and it removes a dependency.

## Implementation Notes
- Replace Escort::App.create block with OptionParser
- Handle --version, --help, --verbose flags
- Accept multiple file arguments
close_reason: Replaced Escort with OptionParser (stdlib). Removed escort, logger, and reline dependencies.

title: Fix class variable state bug in SasProcessor
description:
The `@@mode` class variable in SasProcessor causes state to be shared across instances.

## Problem
Line 34 in sas_processor.rb:
\`\`\`ruby
@@mode = :skip
\`\`\`

This means if you try to process two files concurrently (or even sequentially without creating new instances), the mode state is shared and can cause incorrect behavior.

## Solution
Change `@@mode` to `@mode` (instance variable) and initialize it in the constructor or at the start of the `lines` method.

## Files to Change
- `lib/sas2yaml/sas_processor.rb`
close_reason: Changed @@mode class variable to @mode instance variable to fix shared state bug

title: Add better error messages for parsing failures
description:
When parsing fails, users get cryptic Ruby eval errors with no context about what went wrong.

## Current Behavior
Errors like:
\`\`\`
SyntaxError: (eval):46: syntax error, unexpected instance variable
\`\`\`

## Desired Behavior
\`\`\`
ParseError: Failed to parse 'input.sas' at line 45
  Unexpected content after INPUT statement: "label PDE_ID = ..."
  
  Context:
    43:   @00360 SUBMSN_CLR_CD  $char2.
    44:   ;
  > 45:   label PDE_ID = "Encrypted 723 PDE ID"
\`\`\`

## Implementation Notes
- Wrap eval in begin/rescue
- Track line numbers during processing
- Create custom exception class with context
- Include relevant lines from original file in error message
close_reason: Added ParseError class with context, modified SasProcessor to track line mappings, and updated Sassifier to catch eval errors and provide helpful messages with file path, line number, and context lines

title: Add output format options (JSON, etc.)
description:
Currently only outputs YAML. Additional formats could be useful.

## Proposed Formats
- **JSON** - For programmatic consumption
- **CSV** - For spreadsheet users (field_name, column, length, type)
- **SQL** - Generate CREATE TABLE statement
- **Ruby** - Generate Struct or class definition

## CLI Interface
```
sas2yaml --format yaml input.sas   # default
sas2yaml --format json input.sas
sas2yaml --format csv input.sas
sas2yaml --format sql --table-name patients input.sas
```

## Priority
Medium-low - YAML covers most use cases

title: Replace puts statements with configurable logger
description:
Replace scattered `puts` statements with a proper logger that can be controlled via CLI flags.

## Current State
- `sassifier.rb:11` - prints generated code
- `sas_processor.rb:121,123` - prints array name info
- `sas_processor.rb:171` - prints lrecl match data

## Desired Behavior
- Use Ruby's Logger class
- Default to quiet (no output unless errors)
- Add `--verbose` / `-v` flag to enable debug output
- Logger should go to stderr so stdout can be used for actual output

## Implementation Notes
- Create a module-level logger accessor
- Replace all `puts` with `logger.debug` or `logger.info` as appropriate
- Wire up CLI flag in assemble_command.rb

title: Add clean programmatic API
description:
Currently using the gem as a library requires manual wiring:

\`\`\`ruby
processor = SasProcessor.new(file)
code = processor.lines.join("\n")
sassifier = Sassifier.new(code)
result = sassifier.hash
\`\`\`

## Desired API
\`\`\`ruby
# Parse a file
result = Sas2Yaml.parse("input.sas")

# Parse a string
result = Sas2Yaml.parse_string(sas_content)

# Get structured output
layout = Sas2Yaml::Layout.from_file("input.sas")
layout.fields       # => Array of Field objects
layout.record_length
layout.to_yaml
layout.to_hash
\`\`\`

## Implementation Notes
- Add convenience methods to the Sas2Yaml module
- Consider creating a Layout class that wraps the result
- Keep backward compatibility with existing classes
close_reason: Added clean programmatic API with Sas2Yaml.parse, Sas2Yaml.parse_string, Layout class, and Field class

title: Extract and include labels from SAS files
description:
The parser stops at the label statement but doesn't extract the labels. These could be useful as field descriptions.

## Current Behavior
Parser breaks when it hits label - the label text is discarded.

## Desired Behavior
Parse the label section and include descriptions in output:

```yaml
:patient_id:
  :column: 1
  :name: patient_id
  :type: :string
  :length: 15
  :format: '$char15.'
  :description: "Patient ID (for either Cancer or Non-Cancer Patients)"
```

## Implementation Notes
- Continue parsing after INPUT statement ends
- Match label statements like: FIELD_NAME = "Description"
- Handle multi-line labels
- Labels are optional - not all SAS files have them
